coin_profile.name: Quant
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Quant (QNT) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #63 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Margin of safety present — price near or below speculative fair value during accumulation (bear market) phase.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 5.2/10 and survivability 63.3%. Base: 7.0% p.a., Bull: 13.0% p.a., Bear: -21.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.2/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-21.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 63.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

watchlist_signal.reason: Quality Cash-Flow Protocol with a margin of safety present — price near speculative fair value during a constructive cycle phase.

Any positive news catalyst — exchange listing, partnership, or protocol upgrade — could trigger a sharp near-term rally from current levels
Crypto market sentiment is cyclical — Quant's short-term setup may improve as macro uncertainty fades
bear_points: Broader crypto market volatility and macro headwinds present near-term downside risk
Regulatory developments in major markets could create short-term selling pressure
Without a near-term catalyst, Quant may continue to consolidate or drift lower

bull_points: Over 100% of the maximum supply is already in circulation — future dilution risk is minimal for long-term holders
As the crypto ecosystem matures, Quant's established position should compound in value
Continued development activity and ecosystem growth support a constructive long-term view
bear_points: No multi-year fee data exists for Quant — the protocol's long-term economic durability remains unproven
Technology or regulatory disruption risk cannot be ruled out over a 3-5 year horizon
Long-term execution risk remains a key variable for any crypto investment

metric_assessments.fee_growth: Quant scores 5/10 on fee growth, indicating modest revenue generation relative to its market cap. The protocol is collecting fees, but the volume-to-market-cap ratio suggests transaction activity is not accelerating fast enough to materially increase income. This middling score reflects a protocol that hasn't yet proven it can scale earnings alongside user adoption—a critical benchmark for protocols claiming cash-flow value.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: The score of 5/10 places Quant in the middle tier by market-cap ranking, meaning it's neither deploying capital exceptionally well nor wasting it. At rank #63, the protocol sits in a crowded competitive zone where efficiency gains require either stronger network effects or clearer competitive advantages. Without standout metrics in fee monetization or user retention, capital efficiency remains a question mark rather than a selling point.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: Scoring 5/10 on holder value accrual suggests limited direct benefit flowing back to token owners through buybacks, staking rewards, or other mechanisms. The protocol appears to generate some value but hasn't established a clear and documented path for holders to participate in earnings—a red flag for protocols positioning themselves as cash-flow assets. This gaps the bridge between protocol revenue and token holder returns.
metric_assessments.network_demand: At 2/10, this is Quant's critical weakness: only 1.34% of market cap trades monthly, indicating very thin transaction volume relative to the asset's size. For a cash-flow protocol, this ratio is telling—the network is not being actively used, which means fees aren't accumulating and the fundamental premise of income-generating assets breaks down. This low utilization score is the primary reason for the speculative classification.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: Quant scores a perfect 10/10 here with 99.53% of tokens already in circulation, meaning there is virtually no additional supply overhang to worry about. This tight supply control prevents future dilution from eroding token holder value and removes a common source of downward price pressure. However, excellent supply discipline cannot offset weak network demand or unclear fee accrual mechanisms.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: Scoring 4/10, Quant's rank (#63 by market cap) places it outside the proven top-tier protocols but still established enough to have meaningful adoption. The lower maturity score reflects that the protocol hasn't reached the scale or proven resilience of category leaders, meaning technology risk and market acceptance remain open questions. This is neither a cutting-edge experiment nor a battle-tested blue chip.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Quant earns a Speculative rating due to a 5.2/10 quality score driven by the critical failure of network demand (2/10 utilization) and unproven holder value accrual (5/10), despite excellent dilution control. The protocol lacks demonstrated cash flow generation—with negligible monthly volume relative to market cap—making it too risky to classify as a reliable income asset. Strong supply discipline alone cannot justify the valuation without active network use and clear fee distribution to holders.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Quant is a mid-tier protocol (#63) positioning itself as a cash-flow asset but struggling with the most fundamental metric: actual network usage. With only 1.34% monthly volume-to-market-cap, the protocol is not generating material fees to justify its $1.1B valuation, and it has not established a credible path for token holders to participate in any earnings that do accrue. The tight token supply (99.53% circulating) eliminates dilution risk and shows sound tokenomics discipline, but this strength is overwhelmed by questions about whether the network will ever achieve the adoption needed to justify its cost basis. Unless Quant can demonstrate a significant uptick in transaction volume and a transparent mechanism to return fees to holders, it remains a speculative bet on future protocol relevance rather than a cash-flow investment with current income characteristics.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Quant scores 5.2/10 (Speculative) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

<cite index="1-10,1-28,1-29">Enhanced AI Explainability feature (Mid-2026) will increase transparency by showing the reasoning behind AI governance recommendations, directly addressing a key barrier to institutional adoption and driving higher DAO demand</cite>.
bear_points: <cite index="2-1,2-2">Token unlocks releasing 0.61%-24.58% of market value between December 2025 and January 2026 could increase sell pressure if demand doesn't scale proportionally, with 30.6% of the 10B max supply currently circulating</cite>.
<cite index="2-6">RWA adoption requires navigating complex regulations, creating execution risk that could delay institutional pilots planned for late 2026 and limit near-term adoption growth</cite>.
<cite index="2-12,2-13">Q faces headwinds during AI narrative cooling, while its governance focus versus pure AI speculation may limit short-term hype-driven momentum and price appreciation</cite>.

bull_points: <cite index="2-4,2-5">Partnerships like the Bitget Wallet card and collaborations with protocols like Gimo Finance bridge DeFi and traditional finance, positioning RWA adoption to drive long-term demand for Q's governance and fee utilities</cite>.

<cite index="1-15,1-16,1-17,1-18">RWA positioning is infrastructure not merely a feature, with Quack AI essential to how real capital enters Web3, suggesting long-term utility and demand beyond speculative narratives as institutional adoption compounds</cite>.

<cite index="5-10,5-11,5-12">As the governance layer for the Agent Economy, Quack AI's x402 standard enables sign-to-pay gasless transactions and policy-enforced automation across blockchains, creating structural network effects as AI agent adoption accelerates</cite>.
bear_points: <cite index="5-20,5-21,5-22">With a fully diluted valuation of $219.8M against a current market cap of $64.7M, the 2.55x FDV-to-MC ratio signals substantial token unlock pressure over multiple years that could persistently weigh on price as emission schedules materialize</cite>.

<cite index="2-14,2-15,2-16">Q's price hinges on balancing RWA adoption against token supply inflation and sector sentiment, with execution risk persisting—RWA institutional pilots (Q4 2026) must attract sufficient capital to offset unlock-driven sell pressure</cite>.

As a Cash-Flow Protocol lacking multi-year fee data, Quack AI's long-term economic durability and revenue sustainability remain unproven, creating structural uncertainty around whether governance fees will justify the token's valuation as the protocol matures.

coin_profile.name: Quantix Finance
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Quantix Finance (QFI) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #431 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 3.2/10 and survivability 23.3%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -48.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (3.2/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-48.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 23.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) did not contribute to the median: the implied fair value fell outside the 0.1x–10x price bounds (volume is very low or very high relative to circulating supply). Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Historical price data unavailable for this token. Forecast chart shows projected values only.

metric_assessments.fee_growth: The score of 5 reflects that fee collection mechanisms are either not yet generating meaningful revenue or lack transparent documentation—this tokenized ETF structure doesn't have the typical protocol fee engine that would drive sustainable token value. Without visible annual fees or a clear monetization path tied to token utility, there's no demonstrated way the protocol captures value as it scales, leaving holders dependent on price appreciation alone rather than cash flow generation.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: The score of 5 indicates the protocol is not yet efficient at converting inflows into productive economic activity or token holder benefits. As a rank-based proxy, sitting at #487 suggests QQQON competes in a crowded space without a clear advantage in deploying capital toward differentiated outcomes, meaning each dollar of market cap isn't generating outsized returns relative to peers.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: The score of 5 signals minimal or unclear mechanisms for token holders to capture protocol economics or earnings—whether through staking, governance participation, or fee distribution. The tokenization of a traditional ETF (Invesco QQQ) lacks the organic value accrual typical of native crypto protocols, making the token feel more like a wrapper than a participatory asset where holders directly benefit from protocol growth.
metric_assessments.network_demand: The score of 6 with 6.66% volume-to-market-cap ratio shows modest trading activity relative to the asset's size, indicating baseline interest but not the robust ecosystem engagement that would sustain long-term demand. This level of turnover suggests the token is primarily held rather than actively used, which limits evidence of genuine protocol utility beyond ownership.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: The score of 10 reflects perfect execution here—100% of tokens are in circulation with no hidden supply overhang, meaning there's zero dilution risk from future emission schedules. This transparency is a genuine strength, as it removes a major category of downside risk that plagues many protocols with vesting schedules or uncirculated allocations.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: The score of 1 at rank #487 reveals this is an extremely early or niche protocol with minimal market validation compared to established peers. This low ranking indicates the token has not yet proven its staying power in the market, raising questions about whether the tokenized ETF model has real demand or will fade as a passing experiment in blockchain infrastructure.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: QQQON receives a Dangerous classification because it scores critically low on protocol_maturity (rank #487), fee_growth, capital_efficiency, and holder_value_accrual—all scoring 5 or below—indicating minimal evidence of sustainable tokenomics or cash flow. While dilution control is perfect and network demand shows modest activity, the absence of meaningful economic mechanisms for token holders, combined with unproven market traction, creates substantial downside risk with limited upside visibility.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Invesco QQQ ETF (Ondo Tokenized ETF) is an experiment in bringing traditional finance assets onto blockchain via tokenization, backed by the credibility of a major asset manager but fundamentally untested as a standalone token protocol. The core appeal is simplicity and regulatory clarity—a wrapped version of the Nasdaq-100 ETF with zero token inflation—but this strength is offset by a critical weakness: there is no economic reason to hold or use the token beyond speculative price appreciation. With a $50.7M market cap, immature ranking at #487, and no fee-sharing or utility mechanisms driving value accrual to holders, QQQON remains a structural solution in search of a problem; investors are essentially betting on adoption of tokenized ETFs as an asset class, not on protocol economics. The 9.5% gain over 30 days and perfect supply discipline are footnotes compared to the fundamental challenge: why would someone prefer a tokenized QQQON when they can buy the actual QQQ ETF or hold NASDAQ exposure through traditional or existing crypto channels? This is a speculative bet on institutional adoption of blockchain plumbing rather than a wealth-generating protocol.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Invesco QQQ ETF (Ondo Tokenized ETF) scores 4.8/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.
